definition: The WhatsApp Business API requires all outbound messages sent outside the 24-hour customer service window to use pre-approved message templates. Each template must be classified into one of three categories -- Marketing, Utility, or Authentication -- and submitted to Meta for review before use. Misclassifying a template (e.g., putting promotional content in a Utility template) triggers automatic reclassification to the higher-priced Marketing tier, plus potential account restrictions.
target_audience: Mid-to-large businesses using the WhatsApp Business API through a BSP. Not relevant for WhatsApp Business App users, who do not use templates. WordPress/WooCommerce store owners sending order notifications, abandoned cart messages, or promotional campaigns via the API need to understand this system to control costs.
key_features: Three template categories: Marketing (promotions, offers, product launches), Utility (order confirmations, shipping updates, payment receipts), and Authentication (OTPs, login codes, account verification).
Templates submitted through WhatsApp Manager or BSP dashboards and reviewed by Meta within up to 24 hours.

Per-message pricing as of July 1, 2025 -- each delivered template message is charged individually based on its category and the recipient's country code. Marketing is the most expensive, Authentication the cheapest.
Category review requests can be submitted via WhatsApp Business Support if you believe your template was miscategorized.
Businesses can request appeal through WhatsApp Manager by selecting the template and clicking 'Request review' with supporting details.
limitations: Approval can take up to 24 hours, which slows down time-sensitive campaign launches.
Meta's automated scanning regularly reviews approved templates and may reclassify them after the fact, changing your cost structure without warning.
As of April 16, 2025, businesses no longer receive a 24-hour advance notice before utility templates are reclassified to marketing -- it happens immediately after a written warning.
Repeated misclassification results in a 7-day suspension of template creation privileges, extendable to 30 days on continued misuse.
Template review requests are also disabled during the restriction period.
Authentication templates have the strictest formatting rules and cannot include any promotional content whatsoever.
pricing: Since July 1, 2025, WhatsApp moved from conversation-based billing to per-message billing. Every delivered template message is now charged individually based on category and recipient country. Marketing templates carry the highest per-message rate, Utility templates cost less, and Authentication templates are the cheapest. Exact rates vary by country (e.g., in North America marketing messages are significantly more expensive than utility). If Meta reclassifies a Utility template as Marketing, you retroactively pay the higher Marketing rate. This makes accurate categorization a direct cost-control measure.
scalability_ceiling: Template messaging tiers start at 250 messages/day (Tier 0) and scale up to 100K+/day. The template approval process itself is not a bottleneck at scale, but misclassification penalties become more costly at high volume since each reclassified message costs more. Businesses sending thousands of messages per day need rigorous internal review processes before submitting templates.
setup_complexity: 3/5. For a WordPress site owner with no developer background, the template system adds moderate operational overhead. You need to write templates following Meta's content guidelines, submit them for approval, wait for review, and monitor for reclassification. Most BSPs (like Interakt, Wati, or Respond.io) provide template management dashboards that simplify submission, but understanding the category rules and writing compliant templates still requires learning the system.
migration_complexity: When migrating from the WhatsApp Business App to the API, you start from scratch with templates -- there is no migration of previous message formats. All templates must be created, categorized, and submitted for approval in the new API environment. Expect 1-3 days for initial template library setup and approvals.
data_compliance: Template content is stored and processed by Meta for review purposes. EU businesses should use an EU-certified BSP to ensure template data is handled in compliance with GDPR. Templates containing personal data placeholders (like customer names or order numbers) should be designed to minimize data exposure.
verification_path: Template approval is separate from business verification, but having a verified Meta Business account (green tick / OBA blue tick) can improve template approval rates and reduce review times. Unverified businesses may experience stricter scrutiny on Marketing templates.
message_template_rules: Templates must be categorized as Marketing, Utility, or Authentication at submission. Marketing covers promotions, offers, re-engagement, and product announcements. Utility covers transactional messages like order status, payment confirmations, and shipping updates -- these must not contain any promotional content, CTAs to purchase, or discount codes. Authentication covers one-time passwords and verification codes with the strictest formatting requirements. Meta uses automated scanning to verify categorization post-approval and will reclassify non-compliant templates to the correct (typically higher-priced) category.
messaging_limits: Template messaging is subject to the business's messaging tier. Tier 0 allows 250 unique contacts/day, scaling through Tier 1 (1K), Tier 2 (10K), Tier 3 (100K), and unlimited. Marketing messages are additionally subject to a frequency cap of roughly 2 marketing messages per user per day to prevent spam. Post-October 2025, limits are applied at the portfolio level across all numbers in a WhatsApp Business Account.
ai_automation_policy: AI-generated template content is permitted, but the templates themselves must still go through Meta's approval process. Since January 15, 2026, general-purpose AI chatbots are banned on the API. However, structured AI workflows that generate and send approved templates (e.g., automated order confirmations, support triage responses) are still allowed as long as they use pre-approved template formats.
quality_risk: Template quality is tracked through Meta's quality rating system (Green/Yellow/Red). Consistently low engagement or high block rates on Marketing templates will push your rating to Yellow or Red, resulting in reduced messaging limits and potential 7-30 day template creation restrictions. Misclassification compounds this risk because reclassified templates may already be underperforming if they were miscategorized to begin with.
ads_integration: Click-to-WhatsApp Ads (CTWA) open a 72-hour free messaging window as of July 2025. During this window, businesses can send any template type (Marketing, Utility, or Authentication) without per-message charges. This makes CTWA a cost-effective way to initiate conversations that would otherwise require paid Marketing templates.
ecommerce_features: Product catalog templates allow businesses to share catalog items directly in chat. WooCommerce store owners can sync their product catalog to WhatsApp through BSPs like Interakt or Hello24, then use Utility templates for order confirmations and Marketing templates for product recommendations. Catalog message templates and quick replies allow customers to browse products without leaving the chat.
wpchat_relevance: WPChat.com focuses on the front-end chat widget experience on WordPress sites -- it does not handle template creation or management. However, WPChat's chat funnels and FAQ automation can reduce the need for template-based outbound messaging by capturing and resolving customer queries on-site before they require an API-driven follow-up message. For WordPress store owners using the API, WPChat complements the template system by handling the inbound side (site visitors clicking the chat widget) while the API handles outbound template messaging.
best_for: Ecommerce businesses sending order confirmations, shipping updates, and promotional campaigns at scale through the WhatsApp API.
Marketing teams running segmented WhatsApp campaigns who need to control per-message costs by correctly categorizing templates.
Businesses in regulated industries that need documented, pre-approved message formats for compliance.
not_suited_for: Solo operators or very small businesses using the free WhatsApp Business App, which does not use the template system at all. If you only respond to incoming customer messages and never initiate outbound campaigns, templates are largely irrelevant to your workflow.
cta_angle: If most of your WhatsApp conversations start from your WordPress site (visitors clicking a chat button), WPChat.com handles that entire inbound flow without requiring templates. It is the natural first step before investing in API template infrastructure for outbound messaging.
